scenario: Proposed rent increase of 5% with 30 days notice.

analysis.scenarioSummary: Scenario involves rent increase with elements of rent increase, notice.
relevantRulePoints: notes: Landlords must give at least 3 full calendar months' notice (approx. 24 days minimum formal notice)., Annual rent increase is capped by the rate set by the BC government., Some properties are exempt from the cap.
potentialIssues: Proposed increase (5%) exceeds the allowed cap (3.5%).
missingInformation: Move-in date or tenancy start date not provided.
Scenario description appears brief; additional facts may improve the analysis.

steps: Identify the current rent amount and the proposed new rent amount.
Calculate the percentage increase from the current rent.
Verify that written notice was provided at least 90 days in advance.
Confirm that the required 12-month interval between increases has elapsed.
Check whether the property qualifies for any exemptions (new construction, subsidized housing).
warnings: This rule has an expired effective date. Verify the current rule before proceeding.
This rule may be outdated. Check for more recent amendments.
assumptions: It is assumed the tenancy is covered by the standard residential rules.
citations: Residential Tenancy Act, SBC 2002, c 78, s 42(1)
Residential Tenancy Regulation, BC Reg 477/2003, s 7

outcome.likelyOutcome: Based on the rule structure, one or more factors in the scenario appear to differ from the structured requirements. The outcome would depend on how those factors are resolved.
factors: The rule structure allows a maximum increase of 3.5%.
The proposed increase appears to exceed the structural cap.
uncertainties: The tenancy start date is not specified, which may affect frequency limits.
alternativePaths: Consider a smaller increase within the allowed limit.
Check whether the property qualifies for an exemption.
